questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a disaster recovery plan?
questions.model_answer: Award 1 mark for stating that it describes how an organization can quickly resume work after an unplanned incident.
questions.section: What is a Disaster Recovery Plan

questions.question: Identify one type of technological hazard that can necessitate a disaster recovery plan.
questions.model_answer: Award 1 mark for any one of the following: power outages, pipeline explosions, transportation accidents.
questions.section: What is a Disaster Recovery Plan

questions.question: What does a cold computing site primarily provide?
questions.model_answer: Award 1 mark for stating it provides power, networking capability, and cooling.

questions.question: Explain the role of detective measures in a disaster recovery plan.
questions.model_answer: Award 1 mark for explaining that detective measures focus on detecting or discovering unwanted events as they happen in real time to enable rapid recovery.

questions.question: How does a disaster recovery plan relate to a business continuity plan?
questions.model_answer: Award 1 mark for stating that a DRP is an essential part of a BCP, or that DR is a subset of business continuity that explicitly focuses on IT systems.
questions.section: What is a Disaster Recovery Plan

questions.question: Describe the difference between Recovery Time Objective (RTO) and Recovery Point Objective (RPO).
questions.model_answer: Award 1 mark for correctly defining RTO as the maximum acceptable downtime for systems/applications AND RPO as the maximum acceptable data loss (or age of data to recover).
questions.section: Here is the typical structure of a DR plan

questions.question: Why is it important to regularly test and maintain a disaster recovery plan?
questions.model_answer: Award 1 mark for explaining that risks are always changing, or that testing evaluates procedures for effectiveness and appropriateness, and maintenance accommodates changes in business processes, technology, and risks.
questions.section: What is a Disaster Recovery Plan

questions.question: Imagine an organization relies heavily on real-time transaction processing. Which type of disaster recovery site would be most suitable and why?
questions.model_answer: Award 1 mark for selecting a 'hot' site AND justifying it by stating it is fully functional, has important data mirrored, or allows for immediate switchover to minimize downtime for time-sensitive operations.
questions.section: Here is the typical structure of a DR plan

questions.question: A small business experiences a ransomware attack that encrypts all its critical data. The business has a disaster recovery plan that includes documented backup procedures. What specific actions would the 'disaster recovery procedures' section of their plan likely outline to address this threat?
questions.model_answer: Award 1 mark for mentioning any two of the following: emergency response to limit damages, last-minute backups (if applicable before full encryption), mitigation, and eradication (specifically for cybersecurity threats like ransomware).
questions.section: What is a Disaster Recovery Plan

questions.question: Consider an organization that has identified its payroll processing as a critical application. If a disaster strikes that prevents access to their primary systems, what aspect of the disaster recovery plan's 'Goals' section would be most directly impacted by this criticality?
questions.model_answer: Award 1 mark for identifying the Recovery Time Objective (RTO) and explaining that it would need to be very low (short) to ensure payroll is processed within its required timeframe, or that the RTO would be set to minimize the delay in processing payroll.
questions.section: Here is the typical structure of a DR plan
